q: It takes a great deal of bravery to stand up to our enemies, but just as much to stand up to our friends.
h:
“It takes a great deal of bravery to stand up to our enemies, but just as much to stand up to our friends.” —
Albus Dumbledore

q: 20 percent of your activities will account for 80 percent of your results.
h:
“20 percent of your activities will account for 80 percent of your results.” —
Brian Tracy

q: Deliberately seek the company of people who influence you to think and act on building the life you desire.
h:
“Deliberately seek the company of people who influence you to think and act on building the life you desire.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Trust in dreams, for in them is the hidden gate to eternity.
h:
“Trust in dreams, for in them is the hidden gate to eternity. ” —
Kahlil Gibran

q: Always show more kindness than seems necessary because the person receiving it needs it more than you will ever know.
h:
“Always show more kindness than seems necessary because the person receiving it needs it more than you will ever know.” —
Colin Powell

q: Happiness is pretty simple: someone to love, something to do, something to look forward to.
h:
“Happiness is pretty simple: someone to love, something to do, something to look forward to.” —
Rita Mae Brown

q: A moving door hinge never corrodes. Flowing water never grows stagnant.
h:
“A moving door hinge never corrodes. Flowing water never grows stagnant.” —
Ming-Dao Deng

q: A life without a cause is a life without effect.
h:
“A life without a cause is a life without effect.” —
Paulo Coelho

q: Knowing is not enough, we must apply. Willing is not enough, we must do.
h:
“Knowing is not enough, we must apply. Willing is not enough, we must do.” —
Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

q: Where the willingness is great, the difficulties cannot be great.
h:
“Where the willingness is great, the difficulties cannot be great.” —
Niccolo Machiavelli

q: If you think you can do a thing or think you can't do a thing, you're right.
h:
“If you think you can do a thing or think you can't do a thing, you're right.” —
Henry Ford

q: Where ever you are, you are one with the clouds and one with the sun and the stars you see. You are one with everything.
h:
“Where ever you are, you are one with the clouds and one with the sun and the stars you see. You are one with everything.” —
Shunryu Suzuki

q: It's the job that's never started that takes the longest to finish.
h:
“It's the job that's never started that takes the longest to finish.” —
J.R.R. Tolkien

q: You purchase pain with all that joy can give and die of nothing but a rage to live.
h:
“You purchase pain with all that joy can give and die of nothing but a rage to live.” —
Alexander Pope

q: All religions, arts and sciences are branches of the same tree.
h:
“All religions, arts and sciences are branches of the same tree. ” —
Albert Einstein

q: When one does not have what one wants, one must want what one has.
h:
“When one does not have what one wants, one must want what one has.” —
Sigmund Freud

q: Conscience is a man's compass.
h:
“Conscience is a man's compass. ” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: Sometimes, the only way to evolve is to open ourselves fully.
h:
“Sometimes, the only way to evolve is to open ourselves fully.” —
Kamal Ravikant

q: You can never cross the ocean unless you have the courage to lose sight of the shore.
h:
“You can never cross the ocean unless you have the courage to lose sight of the shore.” —
Christopher Columbus

q: Avoiding danger is no safer in the long run than exposure.
h:
“Avoiding danger is no safer in the long run than exposure.” —
Helen Keller

q: The attempt to escape from pain, is what creates more pain.
h:
“The attempt to escape from pain, is what creates more pain.” —
Gabor Mate

q: The sculptor produces the beautiful statue by chipping away such parts of the marble block as are not needed - it is a process of elimination.
h:
“The sculptor produces the beautiful statue by chipping away such parts of the marble block as are not needed - it is a process of elimination.” —
Elbert Hubbard

q: In the long run, the sharpest weapon of all is a kind and gentle spirit.
h:
“In the long run, the sharpest weapon of all is a kind and gentle spirit.” —
Anne Frank

q: Be yourself; everyone else is already taken
h:
“Be yourself; everyone else is already taken ” —
Oscar Wilde

q: It is in the darkness that one finds the light.
h:
“It is in the darkness that one finds the light.” —
Meister Eckhart

q: Life is simply what our feelings do to us.
h:
“Life is simply what our feelings do to us.” —
Honore de Balzac

q: In order for you to be the BEST you can be for others, first you must be BEST for yourself.
h:
“In order for you to be the BEST you can be for others, first you must be BEST for yourself.” —
Jeffrey Gitomer

q: If you always do what you've always done, you'll always get what you've always got.
h:
“If you always do what you've always done, you'll always get what you've always got. ” —
Henry Ford

q: Life must be rich and full of loving-it's no good otherwise, no good at all, for anyone.
h:
“Life must be rich and full of loving-it's no good otherwise, no good at all, for anyone. ” —
Jack Kerouac

q: You must live in the present, launch yourself on every wave, find your eternity in each moment. Fools stand on their island of opportunities and look toward another land. There is no other land; there is no other life but this.
h:
“You must live in the present, launch yourself on every wave, find your eternity in each moment. Fools stand on their island of opportunities and look toward another land. There is no other land; there is no other life but this.” —
Henry David Thoreau

q: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
h:
“You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.” —
Marcus Aurelius

q: Don't waste your time in anger, regrets, worries, and grudges. Life is too short to be unhappy.
h:
“Don't waste your time in anger, regrets, worries, and grudges. Life is too short to be unhappy.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: Conquer the devils with a little thing called love.
h:
“Conquer the devils with a little thing called love.” —
Bob Marley

q: We must embrace pain and burn it as fuel for our journey.
h:
“We must embrace pain and burn it as fuel for our journey.” —
Kenji Miyazawa

q: Before you can see the Light, you have to deal with the darkness.
h:
“Before you can see the Light, you have to deal with the darkness.” —
Dan Millman

q: Focus is a matter of deciding what things you're not going to do.
h:
“Focus is a matter of deciding what things you're not going to do.” —
John Carmack

q: Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
h:
“Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall. ” —
Confucius

q: Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.
h:
“Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.” —
Thomas Edison

q: There is no greatness where there is not simplicity, goodness, and truth.
h:
“There is no greatness where there is not simplicity, goodness, and truth.” —
Leo Tolstoy

q: It always seems impossible until it's done.
h:
“It always seems impossible until it's done.” —
Nelson Mandela

q: Remember we're all in this alone.
h:
“Remember we're all in this alone.” —
Lily Tomlin

q: All great truths begin as blasphemies.
h:
“All great truths begin as blasphemies.” —
George Bernard Shaw

q: Freedom is the open window through which pours the sunlight of the human spirit and human dignity.
h:
“Freedom is the open window through which pours the sunlight of the human spirit and human dignity.” —
Herbert Hoover

q: A good character is the best tombstone. Carve your name on hearts, not on marble.
h:
“A good character is the best tombstone. Carve your name on hearts, not on marble.” —
Charles Spurgeon

q: If you are unable to find the truth right where you are, where else do you expect to find it?
h:
“If you are unable to find the truth right where you are, where else do you expect to find it?” —
Dogen

q: When you change what you believe, you change what you do.
h:
“When you change what you believe, you change what you do.” —
Spencer Johnson

q: You can't have anything worthwhile without difficulties.
h:
“You can't have anything worthwhile without difficulties.” —
Harry S. Truman

q: Stop comparing yourself to others and focus on your own improvement.
h:
“Stop comparing yourself to others and focus on your own improvement.” —
Lolly Daskal

q: The smallest act of kindness is worth more than the greatest intention.
h:
“The smallest act of kindness is worth more than the greatest intention. ” —
Kahlil Gibran

q: Someday death will take us to another star.
h:
“Someday death will take us to another star.” —
Vincent van Gogh
